items.title: Repeated sampling and coverage
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  pop["fixed true parameter theta"] --> s1["sample 1"]
  pop --> s2["sample 2"]
  pop --> s3["sample 3"]
  s1 --> ci1["CI 1 contains theta"]
  s2 --> ci2["CI 2 misses theta"]
  s3 --> ci3["CI 3 contains theta"]
items.caption: Each sample yields a different interval; the parameter stays fixed. A 95% procedure captures θ in about 95% of repetitions—not 95% probability for one interval after seeing the data.

items.title: CI construction pipeline
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  data["sample data"] --> est["point estimate"]
  data --> se["standard error"]
  level["confidence level 1 minus alpha"] --> crit["critical value z or t"]
  est --> combine["estimate plus minus crit times SE"]
  se --> combine
  crit --> combine
  combine --> interval["confidence interval L U"]
items.caption: Most frequentist CIs follow estimate ± critical value × standard error, with the critical value set by the chosen confidence level.
